## Monumental Seal Of Fortitude

### Game Description

Monumental Seal Of Fortitude is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 8x8. Players take turns placing stones on empty intersections. The game ends when one player has four stones in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. If all intersections are filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Simplified Version

Here is a simplified version of the game implemented in Ludax:

```ludax
(game "Monumental Seal Of Fortitude"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Scoring

In this version, we add scoring to the game. Players earn points for each line of four stones they create.

```ludax
(game "Monumental Seal Of Fortitude"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover (count (line 4)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Capturing

In this version, we add capturing to the game. Players can capture their opponent's stones by surrounding them.

```ludax
(game "Monumental Seal Of Fortitude"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Forced Pass

In this version, we add a forced pass to the game. Players must pass if they have no valid moves.

```ludax
(game "Monumental Seal Of Fortitude"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Complete Version

Here is the complete version of the game:

```ludax
(game "Monumental Seal Of Fortitude"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (4 4))
            (place P2 (4 5))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (not (line 4))
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

## Simple Sloppy Squid Game in Ludax

### Game Description

Simple Sloppy Squid is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their squid pieces on the board. The goal is to create a line of three or more squid pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Implementation

```ludax
(game "Simple Sloppy Squid"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Scoring and Winning Conditions

To make the game more interesting, let's add scoring and winning conditions. A player wins if they have a line of five or more squid pieces.

```ludax
(game "Simple Sloppy Squid"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 5) 1) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Effects and Constraints

Let's add some effects and constraints to make the game more challenging. When a player places a squid piece, they can capture an opponent's piece if it's adjacent to the newly placed piece.

```ludax
(game "Simple Sloppy Squid"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 5) 1) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

Let's add some start rules to initialize the game. We can place a few squid pieces on the board at the start of the game.

```ludax
(game "Simple Sloppy Squid"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 5) 1) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

This implementation provides a basic version of the Simple Sloppy Squid game in Ludax. You can modify and extend the rules to create a more complex and interesting game.

```ludax
)
```

### Adding End Rules

Let's add an end rule to check if a player has won by having four of their pieces in a row.

```ludax
(game "Sturdy Galago Of Relaxation"
```

## Colossal Mustard Koel Game Implementation

### Game Description

Colossal Mustard Koel is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a size of 8x8. Players take turns placing their mustard seeds on the board. The goal is to create a connected line of seeds that spans across the board horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Game Rules

The game rules are implemented in the ludax language.

```ludax
(game "Colossal Mustard Koel"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 8 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (line 5 horizontal)
                    (line 5 vertical)
                    (line 5 diagonal)
                    (line 5 back_diagonal)
                ) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation of the Rules

*   The game is played on an 8x8 rectangular board.
*   Players take turns placing their seeds on empty spaces on the board.
*   The game ends when a player creates a connected line of 5 seeds horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. That player wins the game.
*   If the board is completely filled and no player has won, the game ends in a draw.

### Adding Additional Rules

To make the game more interesting, we can add additional rules. For example, we can introduce a "mustard bomb" effect that allows a player to remove an opponent's seed from the board.

```ludax
(game "Colossal Mustard Koel"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 8 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
                (effects 
                    (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (line 5 horizontal)
                    (line 5 vertical)
                    (line 5 diagonal)
                    (line 5 back_diagonal)
                ) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation of the Additional Rules

*   When a player places a seed, they can also capture an adjacent opponent's seed, removing it from the board and incrementing their score.

### Further Enhancements

We can further enhance the game by introducing different types of seeds with unique abilities or by adding obstacles to the board.

```ludax
(game "Colossal Mustard Koel"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 8 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (7 7))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
                (effects 
                    (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (line 5 horizontal)
                    (line 5 vertical)
                    (line 5 diagonal)
                    (line 5 back_diagonal)
                ) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation of the Further Enhancements

*   The game starts with each player having a seed placed on a specific location on the board.
*   The rest of the game remains the same.

This is a basic implementation of the Colossal Mustard Koel game in ludax. You can modify and extend the rules to create a more complex and interesting game.

## Rustling Cinnamon Cow Game Implementation

### Game Description

Rustling Cinnamon Cow is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a set of cows and cinnamon sticks. The objective of the game is to collect and trade cows and cinnamon sticks to earn points.

### Game Rules

The game rules are as follows:

* The game is played on a 4x4 rectangular board.
* Each player starts with 2 cows and 2 cinnamon sticks.
* Players take turns placing a cow or a cinnamon stick on an empty space on the board.
* A player can trade a cow and a cinnamon stick for 3 points.
* A player can also collect 2 cows or 2 cinnamon sticks in a row, column, or diagonal to earn points.
* The game ends when all spaces on the board are filled or a player reaches 10 points.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Rustling Cinnamon Cow"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 4 4))
        (regions 
            (define cow (pattern (1 1)))
            (define cinnamon (pattern (1 2)))
        )
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1) cow)
            (place P1 (2 2) cinnamon)
            (place P2 (3 3) cow)
            (place P2 (4 4) cinnamon)
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (or 
                            (exists (adjacent cow))
                            (exists (adjacent cinnamon))
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
            (once_through (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent cow))))
                    (result 
                        (exists (adjacent cinnamon))
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover 3)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

The game is implemented using the Ludax language. The game board is a 4x4 rectangular board. Each player starts with 2 cows and 2 cinnamon sticks. The game rules are implemented using the `play` and `end` phases.

In the `play` phase, players take turns placing a cow or a cinnamon stick on an empty space on the board. The `result` constraint checks if the placed piece is adjacent to an existing cow or cinnamon stick. If it is, the player earns 1 point.

In the `once_through` phase, players can trade a cow and a cinnamon stick for 3 points.

The game ends when a player reaches 10 points or when all spaces on the board are filled.

### Incremental Additions

To make the game more complex, we can add additional rules such as:

* Allowing players to move existing pieces on the board.
* Introducing new pieces with different abilities.
* Adding obstacles or challenges to the board.

These additions can be implemented using the Ludax language by modifying the `play` and `end` phases.

### Future Work

To further develop the game, we can:

* Implement a more sophisticated AI opponent.
* Add a graphical user interface to the game.
* Create a web-based version of the game.

These additions can be implemented using a combination of Ludax and other programming languages.

## Nippy Weightless Limpet Game Implementation

### Game Description

Nippy Weightless Limpet is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 5x5. Players take turns placing their limpet pieces on empty squares. The goal is to create a line of 3 or more limpet pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Implementation

```ludax
(game "Nippy Weightless Limpet"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

Let's add a start rule to place the first player's limpet piece at the center of the board.

```ludax
(game "Nippy Weightless Limpet"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Play Effects

Let's add a play effect to capture an opponent's limpet piece when a player places a new piece adjacent to an opponent's piece.

```ludax
(game "Nippy Weightless Limpet"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding End Rules

Let's add an end rule to check if a player has won by creating a line of 3 or more limpet pieces.

```ludax
(game "Nippy Weightless Limpet"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (line 2) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (line 2) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Diamond Shark Of Essence

### Game Description

Diamond Shark Of Essence is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a diamond-shaped grid. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board, trying to capture their opponent's pieces or block their movements.

### Simplified Rules

The simplified rules of the game are as follows:

* The game is played on a 5x5 square board with a diamond-shaped grid.
* Each player starts with 5 pieces placed on their respective corners of the board.
* Players take turns placing one piece on an empty space on the board.
* A player can capture an opponent's piece by placing one of their pieces on an adjacent space.
* The game ends when one player has no pieces left on the board or when all spaces on the board are filled.

### Ludax Implementation

Here is a simplified implementation of the game in Ludax:
```ludax
(game "Diamond Shark Of Essence"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P1 (1 5))
            (place P1 (5 1))
            (place P1 (5 5))
            (place P2 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 4))
            (place P2 (4 2))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (not (exists (occupied mover))) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```
This implementation defines the game board, the starting positions of the pieces, and the basic rules of the game.

### Adding More Rules

To make the game more interesting, we can add more rules, such as:

* A player can only capture an opponent's piece if it is on an adjacent space.
* A player cannot place a piece on a space that is already occupied by one of their own pieces.

Here is an updated implementation of the game with these additional rules:
```ludax
(game "Diamond Shark Of Essence"
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 5))
    )

    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P1 (1 5))
            (place P1 (5 1))
            (place P1 (5 5))
            (place P2 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 4))
            (place P2 (4 2))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result
                        (not (exists (occupied mover)))
                    )
                    (effects
                        (capture (adjacent (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end
            (if (not (exists (occupied mover))) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
```
This updated implementation adds the rules for capturing opponent's pieces and blocking movements.

### Further Improvements

There are many ways to further improve the game, such as:

* Adding more complex rules for capturing pieces
* Introducing new pieces with special abilities
* Creating a more complex board with different types of spaces

These improvements can be implemented by adding more rules and mechanics to the game, using the Ludax language.

The game ends when one player has four pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. If all squares are filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Dainty Quoll Of Pleasure"

        (board (square 4))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding a Start Rule

Let's add a start rule that places a piece for each player at a specific position on the board.

```ludax
(game "Dainty Quoll Of Pleasure"

    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (3 3))

            (repeat (P1 P2)

        (end

        )
```

### Adding a Capture Effect

Let's add a capture effect that removes an opponent's piece when a player places a piece adjacent to it.

```ludax
(game "Dainty Quoll Of Pleasure"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 4))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (3 3))

        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding a Win Condition for a Specific Pattern

Let's add a win condition for a player who has three pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally, and an adjacent empty square.

```ludax
(game "Dainty Quoll Of Pleasure"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 4))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (3 3))

                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )

            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Stylish Peridot Condor Game Implementation

### Game Description

Stylish Peridot Condor is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 5x5. Players take turns placing peridot pieces on empty squares. The game ends when one player has four peridot pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. If all squares are filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Stylish Peridot Condor"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Rule Additions

#### 1. Peridot Pieces Have Different Colors

```lark
(game "Stylish Peridot Condor"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 blue)
        (color P2 red)
    )
)
```

#### 2. Players Can Only Place Peridot Pieces on Squares With Certain Pattern

```lark
(game "Stylish Peridot Condor"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (pattern (1 1))))
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 blue)
        (color P2 red)
    )
)
```

#### 3. Capturing Opponent's Peridot Pieces

```lark
(game "Stylish Peridot Condor"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 blue)
        (color P2 red)
    )
)
```

## Warm Raspberry Lemur Game Implementation

### Game Description

Warm Raspberry Lemur is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their lemur pieces on the board. The goal is to create a line of three or more lemurs in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Initial Implementation

Here's a simplified version of the Warm Raspberry Lemur game implemented in Ludax:

```ludax
(game "Warm Raspberry Lemur"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

Let's add a start rule to place the first player's lemur in the center of the board:

```ludax
(game "Warm Raspberry Lemur"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Effects

Let's add an effect to capture an opponent's lemur when a player places a lemur adjacent to an opponent's lemur:

```ludax
(game "Warm Raspberry Lemur"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Rendering Details

Let's add rendering details to display the players' lemurs with different colors:

```ludax
(game "Warm Raspberry Lemur"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

This implementation provides a basic structure for the Warm Raspberry Lemur game. You can further enhance the game by adding more rules, effects, and rendering details as needed.

prompt:
Invent simple rules for a novel two player abstract strategy game called Convivial Benign Sawfish. Implement it in the ludax language. You will find attached the ludax's grammar as well as a few examples of games implemented in ludax. Start by implementing a simplified version of your rules, and then incrementally add rules that are harder to express in ludax. At each step, make sure you write a compilable game according to ludax's grammar.

        grammar.lark:
// ---Root---
game: "(" "game" name players equipment rules rendering? ")"

// ---Players---
players: "(" "players" positive_int ")"

// ---Equipment---
equipment: "(" "equipment" board ")"
board: "(" "board" (board_square | board_rectangle | board_hexagon | board_hex_rectangle) ")"
board_square: "(" "square" number ")"
board_rectangle: "(" "rectangle" number number ")"
board_hexagon: "(" "hexagon"  number ")"
board_hex_rectangle: "(" "hex_rectangle" number number ")"

// ---Rules---
rules: "(" "rules" start_rules? play_rules end_rules ")"

// ---Start rules---
start_rules: "(" "start" start_rule+ ")"
start_rule: start_place
start_place: "(" "place" player_reference pattern_arg ")"

// ---Play rules---
play_rules: "(" "play" play_phase+ ")"
play_phase: phase_once_through | phase_repeat
phase_once_through: "(" "once_through" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
phase_repeat: "(" "repeat" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
play_mover_order: "(" player_reference+ ")"

play_mechanic: play_place force_pass? 
play_place: "(" "place" mover_reference? place_destination_constraint place_result_constraint? play_effects?")"
force_pass: "(" "force_pass" ")"

// ---Constraints---
place_destination_constraint: "(" "destination" super_mask ")"
place_result_constraint: "(" "result" super_predicate ")"

// ---Effects---
play_effects: "(" "effects" play_effect+ ")"
play_effect: effect_capture 
           | effect_flip
           | effect_increment_score
           | effect_set_score

effect_capture: "(" "capture" super_mask mover_reference? increment_score_arg? ")"
effect_flip: "(" "flip" super_mask mover_reference? ")"
effect_increment_score: "(" "increment_score" mover_reference function ")"
effect_set_score: "(" "set_score" mover_reference function ")"

// ---Functions---
function: function_add
        | function_connected
        | function_constant
        | function_count
        | function_line
        | function_multiply
        | function_score
        | function_subtract

function_add: "(" "add" function+ ")"
function_connected: "(" "connected" multi_mask_arg mover_reference? direction_arg? ")"
function_constant: positive_int
function_count: "(" "count" super_mask ")"
function_line: "(" "line" positive_int orientation_arg? exact_arg? ")"
function_multiply: "(" "multiply" function+ ")"
function_score: "(" "score" mover_reference ")"
function_subtract: "(" "subtract" function function ")"

// ---End rules---
end_rules: "(" "end" end_rule+ ")"
end_rule: "(" "if" super_predicate end_rule_result ")"
?end_rule_result: result_win | result_lose | result_draw | result_by_score

// -- Result definitions --
result_win: "(" mover_reference "win" ")"
result_lose: "(" mover_reference "lose" ")"
result_draw: "(" "draw" ")"
result_by_score: "(" "by_score" ")"

// -- Mask definitions --
super_mask: mask | super_mask_and | super_mask_or | super_mask_not
super_mask_and: "(" "and" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_or: "(" "or" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_not: "(" "not" super_mask ")"

mask: mask_adjacent
    | mask_center
    | mask_corners
    | mask_custodial
    | mask_edge
    | mask_empty
    | mask_occupied
    | mask_pattern
    | mask_prev_move

mask_adjacent: "(" "adjacent" super_mask direction_arg? ")"
mask_center: "center"
mask_corners: "corners"
mask_custodial: "(" "custodial" custodial_length_arg mover_reference? orientation_arg? ")"
mask_edge: "(" "edge" edge ")"
mask_empty: "empty"
mask_occupied: "occupied" | "(" "occupied" mover_reference ")"
mask_pattern: "(" "pattern" dimensions_arg pattern_arg rotate_arg? ")"
mask_prev_move: "(" "prev_move" mover_reference ")"

// "Multi-masks" are special keywords that are manually split into multiple
// sub-masks at compile time. This is mostly useful for the "connected" function,
// which expects a list of masks to check for connections between
multi_mask: multi_mask_corners
          | multi_mask_edges
          | multi_mask_edges_no_corners

multi_mask_corners: "corners"
multi_mask_edges: "edges"
multi_mask_edges_no_corners: "edgesNoCorners"

// ---Predicate definitions---
super_predicate: predicate | super_predicate_and | super_predicate_or | super_predicate_not
super_predicate_and: "(" "and" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_or: "(" "or" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_not: "(" "not" super_predicate ")"

predicate: predicate_equals
         | predicate_exists
         | predicate_full_board
         | predicate_function
         | predicate_greater_equals
         | predicate_less_equals
         | predicate_mover_is
         | predicate_passed

predicate_equals: "(" "=" function+ ")"
predicate_exists: "(" "exists" super_mask ")" // technically equivalent to (>= (count mask) 1) 
predicate_full_board: "(" "full_board" ")"
predicate_function: function // special syntax which is equivalent to "(>= function 1)"
predicate_greater_equals: "(" ">=" function function ")"
predicate_less_equals: "(" "<=" function function ")"
predicate_mover_is: "(" "mover_is" player_reference ")"
predicate_passed: "(" "passed" (mover_reference | BOTH) ")"

// Additional (potentially optional) arguments for predicates
custodial_length_arg: ANY | positive_int
dimensions_arg: "(" positive_int positive_int ")"
direction_arg: "direction:" direction
exact_arg: "exact:" boolean
increment_score_arg: "increment_score:" boolean
multi_mask_arg: multi_mask | "(" super_mask+ ")"
orientation_arg: "orientation:" orientation
pattern_arg: "(" positive_int+ ")"
rotate_arg: "rotate:" boolean

// Optional rendering details
rendering: "(" "rendering" rendering_detail+ ")"
rendering_detail: color_assignment

color_assignment: "(" "color" player_reference color ")"

// General-purpose definitions
?number: SIGNED_NUMBER
?positive_int: /[0-9]+/
?boolean: TRUE | FALSE
?edge: TOP | BOTTOM | LEFT | RIGHT | TOP_LEFT | TOP_RIGHT | BOTTOM_LEFT | BOTTOM_RIGHT
?direction: UP | DOWN | LEFT | RIGHT | UP_LEFT | UP_RIGHT | DOWN_LEFT | DOWN_RIGHT | VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?orientation: VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?color: WHITE | BLACK
// ----------------------------

?player_reference: P1 | P2
?mover_reference: MOVER | OPPONENT
name: STRING
variable_name: /\?[a-z][a-z0-9]*/
id: /[a-zA-Z0-9_]+/

// Constants
TOP: "top"
BOTTOM: "bottom"
UP: "up"
DOWN: "down"
LEFT: "left"
RIGHT: "right"
TOP_LEFT: "top_left"
TOP_RIGHT: "top_right"
BOTTOM_LEFT: "bottom_left"
BOTTOM_RIGHT: "bottom_right"
UP_LEFT: "up_left"
UP_RIGHT: "up_right"
DOWN_LEFT: "down_left"
DOWN_RIGHT: "down_right"
VERTICAL: "vertical"
HORIZONTAL: "horizontal"
ORTHOGONAL: "orthogonal"
DIAGONAL: "diagonal"
BACK_DIAGONAL: "back_diagonal"
FORWARD_DIAGONAL: "forward_diagonal"
ANY: "any"
TRUE: "true"
FALSE: "false"
MOVER: "mover"
OPPONENT: "opponent"
P1: "P1"
P2: "P2"
BOTH: "both"
WHITE: "white"
BLACK: "black"

// Lark imports
%import common.ESCAPED_STRING -> STRING
%import common.SIGNED_NUMBER
%import common.WS
%ignore WS


        example games:
        

(game "Complexity Demo" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Four"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 6 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination (and empty (or (edge bottom) (adjacent occupied direction:up)))))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Six"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 6) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Hex"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hex_rectangle 11 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Gomoku" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 15))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5 exact:true) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Pente" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination center))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Reversi" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (28 35))
            (place P2 (27 36))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (exists
                            (custodial any)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)


(game "Tic-Tac-Toe" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 3))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalath" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hexagon 9))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play 
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover lose))
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalax" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 13))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (and 
                            (not (line 5))
                            (not (= (line 4) 1))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 4) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
